mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as long as the rate floor persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to weakening demand signals.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.0% or increases by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE > 7.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices equity valuations downward in anticipation of lower profit margins and revenue growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 1.5% month-over-month or shows negative 3-month trend
regime_hint: demand contraction or consumer spending slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below 100.0 index level combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary conditions where supply constraints limit growth while price pressures erode real returns. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors flee equities in favor of inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.9778 near or below 100.0 baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates spillover effects to construction employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 thousand units or declines more than 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 891.0 thousand units, near or below 900 threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand jobs per month while UNRATE remains elevated above 7.0% signals a stalled labor market recovery. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize that wage pressure relief and consumer spending support are both weakening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100 thousand AND UNRATE > 7.0%
regime_hint: labor market stagnation or jobless recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes where growth deceleration is unexpected, but may predict positive returns in recession regimes where the market reprices expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion or early recession transition
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances during expansion regimes, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.0% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% signals monetary policy lagging inflation dynamics. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate imminent policy tightening and real rate compression that will reduce equity valuations.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation surge
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 68% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 6-month regressions.
